text.title: Agentic RAG Rules and Failure Modes
text.summary: Concrete operational rules, decision thresholds, production gotchas, and failure modes for agentic RAG systems.

rule.statement: Use these default thresholds: relevance grade >= 0.7 → include document; max rewrite attempts = 2; max retrieval iterations = 3-5; verification score >= 0.8 → output; below threshold → regenerate or caveat.
rule.rationale: Beyond 2 rewrites, query drift from original intent exceeds any retrieval gain. Beyond 5 iterations, token cost grows without bound.

rule.statement: Implement parallel retrieval for independent sub-queries — fan out, collect all results, then aggregate. Reduces total latency by 50-70%.
rule.rationale: Sequential sub-query retrieval accumulates round-trip latencies. Independent sub-queries have no data dependency so parallelization is safe.

rule.statement: Set a hard token budget per agentic loop (e.g., 50K tokens). If cumulative tokens exceed the cap, abort and return a partial answer rather than running indefinitely.
rule.rationale: Without a cap, a stuck rewriter loop can generate thousands of tokens per query at unbounded cost.

antipattern.description: Infinite rewrite loop: poorly graded relevance causes the agent to retry indefinitely without converging.
antipattern.reason: Each rewrite degrades from original intent; after 3+ rewrites, the agent may search for something unrelated to the user's question.

antipattern.description: Grader LLM hallucination: the relevance grader marks a document relevant because it shares topic vocabulary, not because it answers the query.
antipattern.reason: LLM-based graders have the same hallucination risks as generation models. A document about "Python async" may score relevant for "Python async error" even if it contains no error information.

antipattern.description: State corruption in multi-agent coordination: if one agent crashes mid-loop and state is not persisted, the orchestrator restarts with partial context.
antipattern.reason: In-memory state is lost on crash. Production pipelines must checkpoint agent state to durable storage (PostgreSQL, Redis) between hops.

antipattern.description: Response verifier treated as a guarantee: verifier LLM itself can fail to detect hallucinations if the hallucinated content is plausibly worded.
antipattern.reason: Treat verifier output as a probabilistic signal, not a fact-check. For high-stakes domains, add a human review checkpoint before returning the final answer.

rule.statement: Log every agent turn with: query, retrieved chunks, grading decision, and rewrite reason. This is mandatory for debugging where the pipeline diverges.
rule.rationale: Without per-turn logs, diagnosing a bad final answer requires reconstructing the full pipeline state from scratch.

rule.statement: For high-stakes domains (legal, medical), insert a human-in-loop checkpoint before the final synthesized answer is returned — surface top-3 candidate answers for expert selection.
rule.rationale: No automated pipeline achieves 100% accuracy. Expert review of candidates is cheaper than the cost of acting on a wrong answer.

text.reference: Query router prompt templates (basic, multi-source, complexity-aware)
